yaml.title:
Mariarosaria
Di Bagno
yaml.subtitle: LSE-Bocconi MSc DD Candidate in European and International Public Policy and Politics |  BA in International Affairs, minors in Economics and Legal Studies JCU |

markdown.markdown:
Hi! I’m **Mariarosaria Di Bagno**, an **MSc Double Degree** student in **European & International Public Policy and Politics** at [Bocconi University](https://www.unibocconi.eu/) and [The London School of Economics and Political Science (LSE)](https://www.lse.ac.uk/).

I graduated from [John Cabot University](https://www.johncabot.edu/)  with magna cum laude with a **BA in International Affairs** and minors in **Economics** and **Legal Studies**. My interests focus on **European governance**, **evidence-based policy**, and the **energy transition**, with a strong motivation to work on projects that combine **rigorous analysis** and **public impact**.

My academic path is driven by a strong interest in how **economic** and **legal frameworks** shape international relations and policy choices. I’m especially motivated by the role of **diplomacy** in building cooperation across institutions and countries, and I enjoy translating complex issues into clear, actionable insights.

Alongside this, I’m passionate about **data analysis** and evidence-based decision-making. I like working with data to understand patterns, evaluate policies, and communicate results in a way that supports real-world impact.

Beyond the classroom, I explore how **sustainability** and **fashion** can intersect to advance **ethical production**, **responsible consumption**, and more sustainable business models. Looking ahead, I’m eager to contribute to projects that combine **public purpose**, **innovation**, and **social responsibility**—bringing curiosity, analytical rigor, and a collaborative mindset.
